english: for expressing a succession of generations or families, the word is gen. written brgyud (brgyud), rarely rgyud (rgyud), e.g. rj)e-btsún slob-rgyúd daṅ bčas-te (rje btsun slob rgyud dang bcas te) his reverence (the venerable divine) with his race of scholars, in as much as the disciples of a saint are frequently called his spiritual children Mil. — 2. treatise, dissertation, Ssk. तन्त्र, also rgyud-sdé (rgyud sde), esp. the necromantic books of the mysticism of later times Was. (184), in four classes, the so-called four classes of Tantras (rgyud-sdé bži (rgyud sde bzhi)): byá-bai rgyud (bya ba'i rgyud), spyód-pai rgyud (spyod pa'i rgyud), rnal-ḃyór rgyud (rnal 'byor rgyud), rnal-ḃyór bla-na-méd-pai rgyud (rnal 'byor bla na med pa'i rgyud); yet rgyúd bži (rgyud bzhi) is also the short title of a medical work consisting of four parts: rtsá-bai rgyud (rtsa ba'i rgyud), bšád-pai rgyud (bshad pa'i rgyud), man-ṅág rgyud (man ngag rgyud), pyí-mai rgyud (phyi ma'i rgyud). — 3. connection, relation, reference, e.g. of a word.(?) — 4. character, disposition of mind, natural quality; heart, soul; rgyud bzáṅ-po (rgyud bzang po) a good disposition, rgyud ṅán-pa (rgyud ngan pa) a bad disposition; rgyud ži-ba (rgyud zhi ba) a mild disposition, good nature, rgyud ǰám-pa (rgyud 'jam pa) a soft temperament Cs.; rgyud ma - rúṅs - pa (rgyud ma rungs pa) a wicked character Thgy.; sem-gyú' (sems rgyud) C., šé-gyú' (shes rgyud) C., Mil., prob. also rig-rgyúd (rig rgyud) Mil., character; *raṅ-rgyúd ṅán-pai gdoṅ-po tul* (rang rgyud ngan pa'i gdong po thul) restrain the demon of your own wicked heart Mil.; of thoughts, feelings, passions, also of a tiṅ-ṅe-ḍzin (ting nge 'dzin) is said: rgyúd-la skye (rgyud la skye) they arise in the soul; rgyud smin (rgyud smin) the mind ripens Mil.; in some phrases and passages it designates a man's whole personality: raṅ-gi rgyud tóg-tu lén-pa (rang gi rgyud thog tu len pa) to take (other people's) sufferings altogether upon one's own person (not merely to heart) Glr.; raṅ-rgyúd-la brtág-pa (rang rgyud la brtag pa), gžan-rgyúd-la sbyár-ba (gzhan rgyud la sbyar ba) to think a matter through in one's own mind, to draw conclusions from an attentive observation of others, Thgy. — Concerning raṅ-rgyúd (rang rgyud), and gžan-rgyúd (gzhan rgyud) (स्वतन्त्र & परतन्त्र) in the more recent philosophical writings, and in medical works, v. Was. — rgyud-čágs (rgyud chags) Tar. 15, 14, acc. to Schf. sentence, thesis, point. — don-rgyúd (don rgyud), sgrub-rgyúd (sgrub rgyud) Mil.?

I. vb., pf. brgyus (brgyus) and brgyud (brgyud), fut. brgyu (brgyu), imp. rgyud (rgyud). 1. to fasten or file on a string, to string, tá-gu-la brgyús-pa (tha gu la brgyus pa) strung, filed on a string Stg.; ysér-nyag-tag yyu brgyús-pa (gser nyag thag g.yu brgyus pa) a gold chain with turquoises inserted Mil. — 2. to pass through or over, to traverse (later literature and col.) mú-ge rgyúd-nas oṅ (mu ge rgyud nas 'ong) famine passes over, prevails in the country Ma.; *náṅ-na naṅ gyúd-de ḍul* (nang na nang rgyud de 'grul) W. he passes from one room to the other, he visits room after room; *nyúṅ-ti-ne gyúd-na gár-la ton* (nyung ti ne rgyud na gar la thon) W. he is passing through Kullu to Gar; lag-grim-gyis brgyús-pas (lag grim gyis brgyus pas) v. grim (grim); yig-nór brgyúd-pa (yig nor brgyud pa) an error in writing has crept in Tar.; stón-pa gsum ras-čúṅ-pa brgyúd-nas zer (ston pa gsum ras chung pa brgyud nas zer) the three teachers, using Ras-čúṅ-pa (ras chung pa) as a go-between, said ..., = they sent word by Ras-čúṅ-pa (ras chung pa) to this effect Mil. II. sbst. and adj. 1. prop. a participle used a. actively; rgyúd-pa (rgyud pa) (or brgyud-pa (brgyud pa)) one that is transmitting knowledge, a teacher; rgyúd-pa bzáṅ-poi byin-rlabs-čan (rgyud pa bzang po'i byin rlabs can) one that enjoys the blessing of having an excellent spiritual teacher Mil.; ṅai rgyúd-pa rdo-rje-očaṅ-čén yin (nga'i rgyud pa rdo rje 'chang chen yin) Mil. (in this instance it would be justifiable to write brgyúd-pa (brgyud pa), and, regarding this as a derivative of brgyúd (brgyud), to translate it with 'ancestor'). — b. used passively: rdo-rje-očaṅ-nas nyams-rtógs rgyúd-pa de nā-ro lags (rdo rje 'chang nas nyams rtogs rgyud pa de nA ro lags) he to whom knowledge was communicated by Dor-je-očaṅ (rdo rje 'chang) is Nāro Mil.; nā-ro čen-poi rgyud-pa (nA ro chen po'i rgyud pa) a scholar of great Nāro Mil. — 2. a derivative of rgyud (rgyud) 2., a Tantrika, a mystic.
a term used in architecture, wall, panel (?).

Ssk. स्रोतस् a continual flowing, the flow, current or stream (seldom river; perh. smig-rgyui rgyun (smig rgyu'i rgyun) Lex. a river seen by a mirage or fata morgana(?); gáṅ-gai rgyun (gang ga'i rgyun) the river Ganges); ču-rgyún-gyis okyér-ba (chu rgyun gyis 'khyer ba) to be carried away by the current; rgyún-du žúgs-pa (rgyun du zhugs pa) v. obrás-bu bži ('bras bu bzhi); frq. fig. túgs-rǰei rgyun (thugs rje'i rgyun) stream of grace Glr., and sim. in some compounds; often in reference to time, hence rgyún-du (rgyun du) continually, perpetually, always, dus-rgyún-du (dus rgyun du) id.; *dhū-gyún ta-bhu jhe śig* (dus rgyun lta bu byed shig) C. make it as usual!
